problem.title: Matrix Chain Multiplication
problem.description: Given a sequence of matrices, find the most efficient way to multiply these matrices together. The problem is not actually to perform the multiplications, but merely to decide in which order to perform the multiplications.
assumptions: Find parenthesisation that minimizes scalar multiplications.

example.explanation: 4 matrices: A(40×20), B(20×30), C(30×10), D(10×30). Optimal: ((AB)C)D = 26000 multiplications.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use interval DP where dp[i][j] stores the minimum scalar multiplications needed to compute the product of matrices i through j. For each interval length and each split point k, the cost is dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] plus the cost of multiplying the two resulting matrices. The optimal parenthesization is built bottom-up from length-2 intervals to length-n.
